content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "field-operations",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 38,
    "domain_depth": 32,
    "data_richness": 25,
    "interaction_quality": 30,
    "ai_integration": 15,
    "mobile_first": 22,
    "performance": 35,
    "typescript_quality": 40,
    "error_handling": 20,
    "realtime": 25,
    "accessibility": 18,
    "security": 30,
    "differentiation": 15,
    "enterprise_readiness": 20
  },
  "overall_score": 26,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C2-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Daily Log page has no loading, error, or empty states",
      "file": "src/pages/DailyLog.tsx",
      "description": "The Daily Log page renders content immediately with no skeleton loader, no error boundary for failed fetches, and no contextual empty state when no logs exist. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires skeleton loaders within 100ms, contextual empty states with CTA, and inline retry on network failure. A superintendent opening this page on a new project sees a blank or broken table.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component renders its content directly without checking loading or error state, and shows no empty state when there are zero daily logs. AFTER: 1) At the top of the return, check if data is loading (isLoading from the hook or local state) and render a skeleton loader matching the page layout: 4 metric card placeholders (height 96px, border-radius 12px, pulsing animation via opacity 0.3 to 0.7 over 1.5s) in a grid row, plus 6 table row placeholders (height 48px each). 2) If there is an error, render an inline error card with the message and a 'Retry' button that calls the refetch function. 3) If data is loaded but the daily logs array is empty, render an empty state with a Calendar icon (from lucide-react, size 48, color theme.colors.textTertiary), the text 'No daily logs yet. The daily log is your project official record.', and a primary CTA button 'Start Today Log' styled with background theme.colors.primaryOrange, color white, borderRadius 8px, padding '10px 20px'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C2-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "ActivityCard and MentionInput lack ARIA attributes and keyboard support",
      "file": "src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx",
      "description": "The ActivityCard uses a plain div as a clickable element with onClick but no role='button', no tabIndex, no onKeyDown handler, and no aria-label. The EntityLabel button inside lacks aria-label. Screen readers cannot identify or interact with these elements. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require keyboard navigability and all interactive elements to have proper ARIA attributes.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx, in the ActivityCard component and the EntityLabel sub-component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The root container div has onClick but no role, tabIndex, or keyboard handler. The EntityLabel button has no aria-label. AFTER: 1) On the root clickable div of ActivityCard, add role='button', tabIndex={0}, aria-label={`Activity: ${item.user} ${item.action} ${item.target}`}, and onKeyDown handler that calls onClick when Enter or Space is pressed (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' '). 2) On the EntityLabel button element, add aria-label={`View ${target}`}. 3) Add a focus style to the root div: outline: `2px solid ${colors.primaryOrange}`, outlineOffset: '2px', applied via onFocus/onBlur state or a CSS-in-JS approach using a focused state variable. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C2-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Safety page and Field Capture page have no mobile responsive layout",
      "file": "src/pages/Safety.tsx",
      "description": "The Safety page uses fixed-width grid layouts (e.g., gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(4, 1fr)') that do not collapse on mobile. At 375px width, metric cards overflow or are too small to read. Touch targets on action buttons are below 44px minimum. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires every page usable at 375px with 44x44px touch targets and metric cards stacking vertically on mobile.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The metric cards grid uses a fixed gridTemplateColumns like 'repeat(4, 1fr)' or 'repeat(3, 1fr)' that does not adapt to screen width. Action buttons have small padding. AFTER: 1) Change the metric cards grid container to use gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr))' so cards stack on narrow screens. 2) Add a media-query-like approach: wrap the page container in a style that sets padding to 16px when window width is below 768px (use a useEffect with window.innerWidth or a CSS approach). Since we use inline styles, add a state variable `const [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(window.innerWidth < 768)` with a resize listener in useEffect, and conditionally set gridTemplateColumns to '1fr' when isMobile is true. 3) Ensure all buttons have minHeight: '44px' and minWidth: '44px' for touch targets. 4) Set the section spacing gap to spacing.md (16px) on mobile instead of spacing.lg. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C2-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Field Capture page missing camera-first workflow and GPS auto-tagging",
      "file": "src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for Field Capture requires: camera-first UX where Capture button opens camera immediately, auto-metadata with GPS and timestamp, and post-capture form overlay. Currently the page shows a static gallery or placeholder with no actual camera integration, no navigator.geolocation call, and no auto-tagging of photos. This is the core value proposition for field users.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The page has no camera integration, no GPS auto-tagging, and likely shows a placeholder or static content. The 'Capture' or 'Add Photo' button opens a file picker without any metadata enrichment. AFTER: 1) Add a prominent 'Capture' button at the top of the page styled as a large primary CTA: background colors.primaryOrange, color white, borderRadius 12px, padding '16px 32px', fontSize 16px, fontWeight 600, minHeight 56px, with a Camera icon (from lucide-react). 2) When clicked, create and click a hidden input element with type='file', accept='image/*', capture='environment' (this opens the native camera on mobile). 3) On file selection, call navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition to get lat/lng. Store the result in component state as captureMetadata: { lat, lng, timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), accuracy }. 4) After capture, show a post-capture overlay card below the button with: a thumbnail of the captured image (using URL.createObjectURL), the GPS coordinates formatted as 'Lat: XX.XXXX, Lng: XX.XXXX', the timestamp, and optional text inputs for Title and Notes. 5) Add a 'Save Capture' button that stores the data (for now, just adds to a local state array and shows a success toast). 6) If geolocation fails, show the coordinates as 'Location unavailable' instead of crashing. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C2-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Safety page uses hardcoded placeholder metrics instead of computed values",
      "file": "src/pages/Safety.tsx",
      "description": "The Safety page metric cards show hardcoded numbers (e.g., 'Days Since Last Incident: 45', 'TRIR: 1.2') that are not computed from actual incident data. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires 'Days Since Last Incident' calculated from actual incidents, TRIR computed as (incidents x 200000 / total hours), and real open corrective action counts. Founder priorities state every metric must be a real calculated number, never a placeholder.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: Metric card values like 'Days Since Last Incident', 'TRIR', 'Open Corrective Actions', and 'Inspections This Week' display hardcoded numbers or static mock values. AFTER: 1) Import or reference the safety-related hooks (useIncidents, useSafetyInspections, or equivalent). If no hook exists, create local mock data arrays with realistic dates and compute from them. 2) For 'Days Since Last Incident': find the most recent incident with severity >= 'medical_treatment' from the data, compute Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(lastIncident.date).getTime()) / 86400000). If no incidents, show 'No incidents recorded' with a green indicator. 3) For 'TRIR': compute (totalRecordableIncidents * 200000) / totalHoursWorked. Use crew data or a sensible default (e.g., 50 workers x 40 hrs/week x weeks elapsed). Format to one decimal. 4) For 'Open Corrective Actions': filter corrective actions where status !== 'closed' and show the count. 5) For 'Inspections This Week': filter inspections where date is within the current Monday-to-Sunday range. 6) Each metric card should show a colored indicator: green for healthy values, amber for warning, red for critical (e.g., TRIR > 3.0 is red, > 2.0 is amber). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Fieldwire dominates field capture with true offline photo + GPS tagging and Apple Pencil markup. Procore Quick Capture converts video to punch items with auto-transcription. SiteSync Field Capture currently has no camera integration, no GPS auto-tag, and no offline capability, making it non-competitive for the superintendent persona. The Safety module lacks OSHA 300 form generation and computed KPIs that Procore Safety offers. Daily Log has no voice-to-text or 'same as yesterday' feature that would match the 90-second submission target.",
  "summary": "Field Operations scores 26/100, the weakest module area. The three pages (Daily Log, Field Capture, Safety) all lack fundamental loading/error/empty states required by acceptance criteria. Field Capture has no actual camera or GPS integration, making it non-functional for its core use case. Safety metrics are hardcoded placeholders rather than computed values. Accessibility is severely lacking with no ARIA attributes or keyboard support on interactive components. Mobile responsiveness uses fixed grids that break at 375px. The five fixes target: (1) Daily Log states, (2) ActivityCard accessibility, (3) Safety mobile layout, (4) Field Capture camera workflow, (5) Safety computed metrics. These are surgical fixes that address the most impactful gaps without adding new features."
}
```
